\noindent We re-run the model assumption tests in the same order used earlier in the chapter: normality first, then heteroskedasticity, and finally autocorrelation.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
sw_stat, sw_p = shapiro_test(ussteamco_mod_5.resid)
print({'shapiro_W': sw_stat, 'shapiro_p': sw_p})
bp_stat, bp_p, _, _ = het_breuschpagan(ussteamco_mod_5.resid, ussteamco_mod_5.model.exog)
print({'bp_lm': bp_stat, 'bp_p': bp_p})
bg_lm, bg_p, _, _ = acorr_breusch_godfrey(ussteamco_mod_5, nlags=3)
print({'bg_lm': bg_lm, 'bg_p': bg_p})
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]

	Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  ussteamco.mod.5$residuals
W = 0.9487536, p-value = 0.0955396


	studentized Breusch-Pagan test

data:  ussteamco.mod.5
BP = 7.036708, df = 5, p-value = 0.217925


	Breusch-Godfrey test for serial correlation of order up
	to 3

data:  ussteamco.mod.5
LM test = 5.199184, df = 3, p-value = 0.15778
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
